content.problemStatement:
In the gym, Chef prefers to lift at least WWW grams during a bench press and if that's impossible, Chef considers his workout to be incomplete and feels bad.

The rod weighs WrW_rWr​ grams and there are NNN other weights lying on the floor that weigh w1,w2,...,wNw_1, w_2, ..., w_Nw1​,w2​,...,wN​ grams. To maintain balance and to ensure that there is no unnecessary load due to torque, it's important that the weights added to the left side are symmetric to the weights added to the right side. It is not required to use all of the weights. It is also not required to use any weights at all, if Chef feels satisfied lifting only the rod.

For example:



111 222 222 111 ∣|∣Rod Center∣|∣ 111 111 111 333 is a wrong configuration, but




111 222 333 111 ∣|∣Rod Center∣|∣ 111 333 222 111 is a right configuration.



Find whether Chef will be able to collect the required weights to feel satisfied.
constraints: Subtasks**Subtask #1 (30 points):** wi=1w_i = 1wi​=1 for all valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line contains three space-separated integers N,W,WrN, W, W_rN,W,Wr​.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers w1,w2,…,wNw_1, w_2, \ldots, w_Nw1​,w2​,…,wN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, output the answer in a single line: "YES" if Chef will be satisfied after his workout and "NO" if not (without quotes).

You may print each character of each string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: Since the weight of the rod is at least the required weight to be lifted, Chef will feel satisfied after the workout.
Test case 2: The configuration having maximum weight is:
101010 101010 ∣|∣Rod Center∣|∣ 101010 101010
So the maximum total weight Chef can lift is 50+4⋅10=9050 + 4 \cdot 10 = 9050+4⋅10=90 which is less than the required amount to get satisfied.
Test case 3: The configuration having maximum weight is:
101010 101010 101010 ∣|∣Rod Center∣|∣ 101010 101010 101010
So the maximum total weight Chef can lift is 40+6⋅10=10040 + 6\cdot 10 = 10040+6⋅10=100 which is equal to the required amount to get satisfied.
